title: Decision making and unintended consequences
description: Practice applying knowledge about economic decision making and unintended consequences.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the process of cost-benefit analysis in economics?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: analyzing the total costs and total benefits of a decision to determine if it should be made
comparing the additional costs of a decision to the additional benefits to decide if the decision should be made
evaluating only the financial costs involved in a decision, ignoring non-monetary benefits
considering only the long-term impacts of a decision without regard to immediate effects

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the purpose of using a PACED decision-making grid in economics?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to identify the fastest way to make a decision without considering any criteria
to compare different alternatives based on a set of weighted criteria to make the most informed decision
to eliminate all possible choices except one, ensuring a single clear option
to focus solely on the financial aspects of a decision, ignoring other factors

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the concept of choices having long-term, unintended consequences in economics?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Every choice made has predictable and immediate outcomes only.
Choices often lead to both anticipated results and unexpected effects that may occur in the long run.
Unintended consequences are always negative and should be avoided in decision-making.
Long-term consequences of choices happen, but can be ignored because they are not significant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jorge is deciding whether to spend an extra hour at the gym, or to use that time to cook a healthy meal at home. 

Activity | Marginal Benefit| Marginal Cost
-|-|-
One hour workout  | Increased strength & endurance, no travel | Time to prepare a meal foregone    
Cooking for one hour | Healthy, nutritional meal | Potential fitness improvement foregone 

**Based on the Cost-Benefit Analysis (CBA) chart, which choice would be more beneficial for Jorge if he prioritizes health over efficiency?**

[[☃ dropdown 1]]

**Based on the choice above, what is the opportunity cost?**

[[☃ dropdown 2]]
hints:
Think about what each activity entails and how it aligns with Jorge's priorities. His priority is health over efficiency.

Efficiency, in this scenario, refers to the fact that he is already at the gym and would not have to travel to it.
Since Jorge values health over efficiency, $\boxed{\text{cooking for one hour}}$ would be the most beneficial choice for him.
Next, we need to determine the **opportunity cost**, which is the next best alternative he had to give up.

By choosing to cook for one hour, Jorge gave up $\boxed{\text{increased strength and endurance}}$. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: one hour workout
cooking for one hour
either one is correct
increased strength and endurance
time to make the meal

assessment_items.question_content:
Maria is deciding whether to use an hour to study or watch a movie and relax. 

Activity | Marginal Benefit| Marginal Cost
-|-|-
One hour studying  | Better understanding of material, improved exam scores | No time to relax 
Watching a movie for one hour | Reduced stress, improved mental well-being | Less potential for academic improvement 

**Based on the Cost-Benefit Analysis (CBA) chart, which choice would be more beneficial for Maria if she prioritizes getting good exam grades over relaxation?**

[[☃ dropdown 1]]

**Based on the choice above, what is the opportunity cost?**

[[☃ dropdown 2]]
hints: Think about what each activity entails and how it aligns with Maria's priorities. Her priority is performing well on her upcoming exam over watching a movie and relaxing.
Since Maria values doing well on the exam over relaxation, $\boxed{\text{one hour studying}}$ would be the best choice for her.
Next, we need to determine the **opportunity cost**, which is the next best alternative she had to give up.

By choosing to study for one hour, Maria gave up time to relax, which means that she will not get the benefits of $\boxed{\text{reduced stress and improved mental well-being}}$ that come with choosing relaxation. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: one hour studying
watching a movie for one hour
either one is correct
reduced stress and improved mental well being
watching an hour long movie
no time to relax

assessment_items.question_content:
Barry is deciding whether to buy a used car or continue using public transportation. 

Factor | Buying a used car| Using public transportation
-|-|-|-|-
Cost  | $\$5{,}000$ (initial purchase) + $\$1{,}500$ (annual maintenance, insurance and fuel)| $\$600$ (annual public transportation pass)
Benefit: Convenience | High - Flexible travel schedule, door-to-door service | Moderate - Fixed routes and schedules, may involve transfers
Benefit: Time savings | High - Direct routes, no waiting for buses/trains | Low - Potential delays, waiting times

**Based on the Cost-Benefit Analysis (CBA) chart, which choice would be more beneficial for Barry if he prioritizes saving money over time?**

[[☃ dropdown 1]]

**Based on the choice above, what is the opportunity cost?**

[[☃ dropdown 2]]
hints: Think about what each activity entails and how it aligns with Barry's priorities. His priority is saving money while commuting.
Since Barry values money, $\boxed{\text{using public transportation}}$ would be the best choice for him, since it costs less than buying and maintaining a car.
Next, we need to determine the **opportunity cost**, which is the next best alternative he had to give up.

By choosing to use public transportation, Barry gave up the opportunity to have a $\boxed{\text{flexible travel schedule and door to door service}}$. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

choices.content: using public transportation
buying a used car
either one is correct
flexible travel schedule, door to door service
potential delays and waiting times

assessment_items.question_content:
Lali is moving into a college dorm. She is trying to decide which method of transportation would work best for her while in college.

|| **PACED decision making** ||
Alternatives | Expenses (1) | Traffic (2) | Travel time (3) | Total score
- | - | - | - | -
Car | $2 \times 1 = 2$ | $3 \times 2 = 6$ | $5 \times 3 = 15$ | $23$ 
Bike | $3 \times 1 = 3$ | $4 \times 2 = 8$ | $5 \times 3 = 15$ | $26$ 
Bus | $5 \times 1 = 5$ | $4 \times 2 = 8$ | $4 \times 3 = 12$ | $25$ 

**Based on the PACED grid above, which is the best choice for Lali?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: car
bike
bus

assessment_items.question_content:
Sally is looking at different college majors. She made a decision based on the grid, and assumed the opportunity cost.

|| **PACED decision making** ||
Alternatives | Future job prospects (1) | Interest in field (2) | Earning potential  (3) | Total score
- | - | - | - | -
Major A (Computer Science) | $5 \times 1 = 5$ | $4 \times 2 = 8$ | $5 \times 3 = 15$ | $28$ 
Major B (Art History)  | $2 \times 1 = 2$ | $5 \times 2 = 10$ | $4 \times 3 = 12$ | $27$ 
Major C (Biology) | $4 \times 1 = 4$ | $3 \times 2 = 6$ | $4 \times 3 = 12$ | $21$ 

**Which alternative is the remaining trade-off?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Major A (Computer Science)
Major B (Art History)
Major C (Biology)

assessment_items.question_content:
Jaime is moving into her own apartment and is trying to decide which method of food preparation would work best for her. 

|| **PACED decision making** ||
Alternatives | Expenses (1) | Traffic (2) | Travel Time (3) | Total Score
- | - | - | - | -
Cooking at home | $3 \times 1 = 3$ | $4 \times 2 = 8$ | $5 \times 3 = 15$ | $26$ 
Meal delivery | $5 \times 1 = 5$ | $5 \times 2 = 10$ | $4 \times 3 = 12$ | $27$ 
Eating out | $4 \times 1 = 5$ | $4 \times 2 = 8$ | $3 \times 3 = 9$ | $21$ 

**If Jaime makes a choice based on the PACED grid above, what will be the opportunity cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: cooking at home
meal delivery
eating out

assessment_items.question_content:
A small town decides to attract more businesses by offering tax incentives to companies that move to the area. 

**While this policy initially boosts local employment and economic activity, what might be an unintended long-term consequence of this decision?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The town's infrastructure, such as roads and schools, will improve due to increased tax revenues.
Local government services, such as public safety and education, might suffer due to decreased tax revenues from the incentives given to businesses.
The town will become a major metropolitan area within a few years due to rapid business growth.
Residents will experience lower property taxes because of the increased economic activity in the town.

assessment_items.question_content:
The city of Richmond, Virginia, recently completed the construction of a new bridge to ease traffic congestion and improve connectivity between different parts of the city. 

**Which of the following is a *positive* unintended consequence of building the new bridge?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Increased noise pollution in nearby residential areas.
A rise in the number of accidents on the bridge due to higher traffic volumes.
More business for local restaurants and shops near the bridge.
Higher maintenance costs for the city due to the new infrastructure.

assessment_items.question_content:
The city of Richmond, Virginia,  decided to convert several abandoned industrial areas into green spaces to increase quality of life and surrounding property values. While both did increase, an unexpected positive outcome was also observed.

**Which of the following is most likely the positive unintended consequence of building the green spaces?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased quality of life
an increase in property values
return of several rare bird species
higher costs for the city for the maintenance of the green spaces

assessment_items.question_content:
A school district decided to implement a policy requiring students to attend additional tutoring sessions after school to improve standardized test scores.

**Which of the following is a likely unintended consequence of this policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Students' standardized test scores will improve significantly across the district.
Students will have less time for extracurricular activities, potentially leading to decreased participation in sports, arts, and clubs.
Parents will save money on private tutoring services as the school provides additional support.
Teachers will receive additional compensation for the extra hours spent on tutoring.
